description_long: This kiosk is a compact, rectangular structure with a utilitarian, industrial appearance, primarily finished in a tan, slightly weathered material. The front features an integrated computer terminal with a screen, keyboard, and speakers, as well as a control panel with buttons and a touchpad, suggesting interactive or transactional use. The sides are solid and unadorned, while the rear panel displays several circular ventilation grilles and two rectangular access hatches, likely for maintenance or service. The top surface is flat and broad, suitable for placing small objects or paperwork. The kiosk's construction appears robust, with visible bolts and reinforced edges, indicating durability for public or outdoor environments. There is no visible entryway or window, and the overall design is functional, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A tan, industrial-style kiosk with an integrated computer terminal, control panel, and rear access hatches, built for durability and public use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the computer terminal, keyboard, speakers, and control panel set into the kiosk's surface.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the kiosk's solid, boxy profile and the depth of the integrated terminal area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple ventilation grilles and two rectangular access hatches, with no entryway or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the kiosk's solid construction and lack of openings.
attribute_signage_type: digital display integrated into the front terminal, displaying text on the screen
attribute_roof_design: flat roof, same material as the body, no overhangs or decorative features
